question: A lent Rs. 5000 to B for a duration of 2 years and Rs. 3000 to C for a period of 4 years both at the same simple interest rate. In total, he received Rs. 2200 as interest from both borrowers. What is the rate of interest per annum?

A person invested Rs18,000 in two schemes — Scheme X at 15% p.a. and Scheme Y at 10% p.a., both under simple interest. After 1 year, the total interest from both schemes was Rs2,160. How much was invested in Scheme X?
In how many years will a sum of Rs. 8,000 give a simple interest of Rs. 3,000 at 12.5% per annum?

Consider the following statements regarding simple interest: 1. The simple interest on Rs10,000 for 3 years at 15% per annum is Rs4,500. 2. If a sum of money doubles in 5 years at simple interest, then it will become 1.5 times in 3 years. 3. The simple interest rate required for an amount to become 4.5 times in 7 years is 50%. Of these statements, which are correct?

Rahul borrowed a sum of money at simple interest of 5% per annum for the first 3 years, 7% per annum for the next 5 years, and 9% per annum for the period beyond 8 years. If he pays a total of Rs6800 as interest only at the end of 10 years, how much money did he borrow?

Aman needed Rs30,000 for a start-up and split the sum between two private lenders under simple-interest terms: Lender A charges 9 % p.a. (simple interest) Lender B charges 6 % p.a. (simple interest) Aman kept both loans exactly 4 years and then made a single lump-sum payment that settled only the accumulated interest, which came to Rs9,600. After preparing his books of account, Aman discovered that he interchanged the principal amounts taken from the two lenders, his four-year interest outgo would have been Rs1,200 less than what he actually paid. How much of the original Rs30,000 did Aman borrow at 9 % p.a. from Lender A?

Sarah invested amounts in three different schemes M, N, and O at simple interest rates of 10% p.a., 12% p.a., and 15% p.a. respectively. If the total interest accrued in one year was Rs2282 and the amount invested in Scheme O was 180% of the amount invested in Scheme M and 250% of the amount invested in Scheme N, what was the amount invested in Scheme N?

An individual invested certain amounts in three different schemes P, Q, and R at simple interest rates of 7% p.a., 9% p.a., and 11% p.a. respectively. If the total interest accrued in one year was Rs2520 and the amount invested in Scheme R was 250% of the amount invested in Scheme P and 300% of the amount invested in Scheme Q, what was the amount invested in Scheme P?
A sum of Rs10000 is lent out in two parts, one at 7% simple interest and the other at 12% simple interest. If the annual interest is Rs900, the sum lent at 12% is:

Rs. 900 is lent at a certain rate of simple interest. After 9 months, another Rs. 600 is lent at a rate that is 1.5 times the original rate. If the total simple interest after 1 year is Rs. 72, find the original rate.
A sum of Rs5000 is lent out in two parts, one at 6% simple interest and the other at 9% simple interest. If the annual interest is Rs390, the sum lent at 6% is:

A man has Rs15,000. He lends a part of it to X for 3 years at 10% simple interest and the remaining to Y for 3 years at 12% simple interest. If the total interest received from both X and Y after 3 years is Rs4,950, find the amount lent to X.
C borrowed Rs6000 from D on 15 March 2023 at a simple interest rate of 10% per annum. C decided to repay the loan on 15 September 2023. How much total amount must C return to D?

A person invested a total sum of Rs2200 in three different schemes of simple interest at 4%, 5%, and 10% per annum. At the end of one year, he got the same interest from all three schemes. What was the amount (in Rs) invested at 5%?
Reena borrowed Rs. 1200 at simple interest for a number of years equal to the rate of interest. At the end of the loan term, she paid Rs. 432 in interest. What was the interest rate?

A person invested Rs20,000 in two different schemes. He invested one part for 4 years at 8% simple interest and the other part for the same duration at 10% simple interest. If the interest earned from the second part was Rs2,240 more than the interest earned from the first part, how much money was invested in the second scheme?

An educational fund of Rs6,00,000 earns simple interest at 9% per annum. This annual interest is used to grant three scholarships. If the first and third scholarships are Rs18,000 and Rs22,000 respectively, what is the value of the second scholarship?

Suppose RsX is invested, and after 2 years at 5% simple interest, it yields RsY interest. If RsY is then invested for 2 years at 5% simple interest, it yields RsZ interest. Which of the following is true?
Sarah lent Rs12000 to John on 10 January 2024 at a simple interest rate of 6% per annum. John plans to pay back the entire amount on 10 July 2024. What is the total amount John needs to return?

If A, B, and C are three amounts of money such that B is the simple interest on A, and C is the simple interest on B, all for the same time and at the same rate of interest, then which of the following is true?
At what rate of simple interest will Rs2,000 become Rs2,520 in 4 years?

A person invested Rs8,000 in one scheme at 6% simple interest and Rs5,000 in another scheme at 8% simple interest for the same duration. If he received a total interest of Rs1,760 from both investments, what was the duration (time period) of the investments?
A sum becomes 2.5 times of itself in 5 years under simple interest. In how many years will it become 5 times itself at the same rate?

A person invested a total sum of Rs1900 in three different schemes of simple interest at 2%, 4%, and 5% per annum. At the end of one year, he got the same interest from all three schemes. What was the amount (in Rs) invested at 4%?
A shopkeeper took a loan of Rs7500 from a bank on 1 April 2025 with simple interest at 9% per annum. He decided to clear the loan on 1 September 2025. How much amount did he pay back to the bank?
A certain sum becomes 4 times itself in 8 years at simple interest. In how many years will it become 12 times itself at the same rate?
If the simple interest on a sum of Rs. A at 6% per annum for 2 years is equal to the simple interest on Rs. B at 4% per annum for 3 years, then what is the ratio of A to B?
If the simple interest on a certain sum of money for 3 years at 6% per annum is Rs360, what would be the simple interest on the same sum for 5 years at 8% per annum?
A sum amounts to Rs6,600 in 2 years and Rs7,200 in 4 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum?
A sum of Rs8000 is lent out in two parts, one at 5% simple interest and the other at 7% simple interest. If the annual interest is Rs500, the sum lent at 7% is:
What will the ratio be of the simple interest earned on a given amount at the same interest rate over 6 years compared to that earned over 9 years?

A donor deposits Rs8,00,000 in a trust that earns 10% simple interest annually. The interest is used to distribute three annual awards. If the second and third awards are Rs35,000 and Rs20,000 respectively, what is the value of the first award?
If P = Rs. 1,500, R = 8.5%, and T = 2.5 years, find the simple interest.
If the simple interest on a certain sum of money for 4 years at 5% per annum is Rs500, what would be the simple interest on the same sum for 6 years at 7% per annum?
Rs. 1500 is lent out at 6 % per annum simple interest for 3 years. Find the amount after 3 years.

The simple interest on a certain sum is one-fourth of the principal, and the number of years is equal to the rate of interest per annum. What is the rate of interest? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]
A sum of money becomes of itself in 5 years at a certain rate of simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]
A sum of money doubles itself in 8 years at a certain rate of simple interest. In how many years will it become five times itself at the same rate? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

Rohan invests a portion of a total sum of P in Scheme A for 4 years and the remaining amount in Scheme B for 5 years. Scheme A offers a simple interest rate of r% per annum, while Scheme B offers a simple interest rate of (r+2)% per annum. The amount invested in Scheme A is 50% more than the amount invested in Scheme B. If the total interest earned from both schemes is Rs1,920 and the amount invested in Scheme A is Rs2,400, what is the simple interest rate, r? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
At simple interest, a certain amount of money doubles in five years. What is the interest rate?
A sum of money doubles itself in 8 years at a certain rate of simple interest. In how many years will it become five times itself at the same rate? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

Rohan invests a portion of a total sum of P in Scheme A for 4 years and the remaining amount in Scheme B for 5 years. Scheme A offers a simple interest rate of r% per annum, while Scheme B offers a simple interest rate of (r+2)% per annum. The amount invested in Scheme A is 50% more than the amount invested in Scheme B. If the total interest earned from both schemes is Rs1,920 and the amount invested in Scheme A is Rs2,400, what is the simple interest rate, r? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

An investor divides a total of $25,000 into two different accounts. Account A earns simple interest at a rate of 4% per year, and Account B earns simple interest at a rate of 6.5% per year. After three years, the total interest earned from both accounts is $3,975. What is the amount of money invested in Account A?
A person borrows Rs20,000 at 8% per annum simple interest for 3 years. What is the total interest he will pay at the end of 3 years?
A sum of Rsy amounts to Rs10,920 at 6% per annum in a time in which a sum of Rs12,000 amounts to Rs15,000 at 5% per annum, both at simple interest. The value of y is:
An amount invested on simple interest at R% p.a. amounts to Rs. 9,504 and Rs. 12,384 in 4 years and 9 years respectively. Find the value of R . [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
A sum of money becomes 240% of itself in 8 years at simple interest. Find the simple interest earned when Rs. 3,000 is invested at the same rate for 3 years. [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

Rohit distributed 70% of the total money he had between Priya and Meena in the ratio 5:9, respectively. Priya and Meena deposited their respective shares at 6% and 10% simple interest per annum for 4 years. If the difference in the interest earned by Meena and Priya is Rs. 2,304, find the total amount Rohit had. [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
A sum was invested at simple interest, and it became (132/115) times of what it had amounted to in 2 years when invested for 5 years. What is the rate of interest?

A man lends out a sum of money by putting 40% of it at 7.5% p.a. simple interest and the remaining 60% at 11.25% p.a. simple interest. After 2½ years (2.5 years) his total interest income is Rs6,825. What was the original sum (principal) he lent?

At 5% p.a. simple interest, the simple interest earned by A on his investment for 4 years equals the simple interest earned by B on his investment for 6 years. If B invested Rs12,000 less than A, what was A's investment (principal)?
A man invests Rs5,000 in a savings account that pays a simple interest rate of 6% per annum. What will be the total amount in his account after 3 years?
A sum of money becomes 5 times itself in 20 years at a certain rate of simple interest. In how many years will it become 9 times itself at the same rate?
If a sum doubles itself in 10 years under simple interest, what is the annual rate of interest?
At a specific rate of simple interest, a sum of money becomes Rs18,500 after four years and Rs19,800 after six. Calculate the simple interest rate at 12% annually for five years on the same amount.

A specific amount of principal is invested by an individual. The annual simple interest rate is 6% for the first two years, 8% for the following four, and 10% for the remaining time. The total interest earned after nine years is Rs7,400. How much was invested initially?
A person takes a loan of Rs25,000 for 2 years at a rate of interest of 5%. What will be the total amount to be paid after 2 years?

Anshu invested a certain sum at a simple interest of r% p.a. such that it amounts to 180% of itself in 5 years. Find the interest earned when Rs. 2500 is invested at a simple interest of r% p.a. for 2 years.

Aman distributed 80% of his total money between Kavya and Aryan in the ratio 3 : 5. Kavya invested her share at 9% p.a. and Aryan at 6% p.a. simple interest for 6 years. If the difference in the interest earned by Aryan and Kavya is Rs. 2,592, then what is the total amount Aman had?
A sum of Rs. 5750 earns Rs. 2620.50 as simple interest in 5 years. Find the approximate rate of interest.
What simple interest rate will cause a sum of money to double in 15 years?
A certain sum amounts to Rs1,260 in 2 years and Rs1,350 in 5 years. Find the principal amount.
A sum of Rs40,000 is borrowed for 4 years at a certain rate of interest. If the total interest paid at the end of 4 years is Rs24,000, what is the rate of interest?
Simple interest on a sum for 6 years is equal to 40 percent of the principal. In how many years interest will be equal to the principal?
On 20 January 2023, Preeti borrowed Rs. 22,400 at 16% p.a. simple interest. She repaid the loan on 20 May 2023. What amount did she repay?
The difference between simple interest on a certain sum at the annual rate of 12 percent for 4 years and 7 years is Rs. 72. What is the sum?

A sum invested at rate r% simple interest for 5 years yields Rs1,500 as interest. If the rate were increased by 2% p.a. (i.e. r+2% p.a.) for the same period, the interest would be Rs1,800. What was the original principal?
In how much time will a sum of Rs. 14000 amounts to Rs. 20720 at the rate of 6 percent per annum at simple interest?
What will be the simple interest on a principal of Rs. 1600 for 8 years at the rate of 2 percent per annum?
If a certain sum of money becomes double of itself in 5 years 6 months at simple interest, then what will be the annual rate of interest (approximate)?
The difference between simple interest on a certain sum at the annual rate of 10 percent for 8 years and 10 years is Rs. 80. What is the sum?

A sum of Rs2,400 doubles in 8 years under simple interest. Another sum Q is invested at the same rate. The interest earned on Q for 5 years exceeds the interest earned on Rs2,400 for 3 years by Rs300. What is Q?
The simple interest on a certain sum of money for 2 years at 6% per annum is Rs1,200. What is the sum of money?
If the sum of money amounts to Rs2000 at the end of 2 years and Rs2500 at the end of 5 years at simple interest, what is the rate of interest?
What will be the simple interest on Rs. 1450 at 9% per annum for 3 years?
A sum of money doubles in 10 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum?
What sum will amount to Rs8800 in 2 years at 10% simple interest per annum?
What will be the simple interest on Rs.1300 at 6% per annum for 4 years?
A sum becomes Rs6840 in 3 years at 9% simple interest per annum. What is the original sum?
A sum of money, when invested at simple interest, amounts to Rs12,000 in 4 years and Rs15,000 in 7 years. What is the original principal amount?
What is the simple interest on Rs5000 for 2 years at 5% per annum?
The difference between simple interest on a certain sum at the annual rate of 15 percent for 6 years and 7 years is Rs. 75. What is the sum?

A sum of Rs10,000 is lent at a certain rate of simple interest. The amount at the end of 5 years is Rs15,000. If the rate of interest were 2% more, what would the simple interest be at the end of 5 years?
In how much time will a sum of Rs. 15000 amounts to Rs. 21000 at the rate of 8 percent per annum at simple interest?
A certain sum of money, at a simple interest rate, becomes 7/5 times itself in 5 years. What is the annual rate of interest?
In how much time will a sum of Rs. 12000 amounts to Rs. 15600 at the rate of 5 percent per annum at simple interest?
If a certain sum of money becomes double of itself in 4 years 4 months at simple interest, then what will be the annual rate of interest (approximate)?
Ajay invests a certain sum of money on simple interest at 8% p.a. for 15 years. If the interest earned by him is Rs. 58,410, then find the sum invested by him.
A sum of Rs. 6250 earns Rs. 976.50 as simple interest in 3 years. Find the approximate rate of interest.
A sum of Rs. 6500 earns Rs. 1602.00 as simple interest in 4 years. Find the approximate rate of interest.
A sum becomes Rs. 11,760 in 6 years at r% simple interest. If the interest rate had been (r + 5)% p.a., the amount would have been Rs. 13,320. Find the principal.
A sum of money amounts to Rs. 6,720 in 4 years and to Rs. 7,680 in 6 years at a certain rate of simple interest. What is the annual rate of interest? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 2]
A sum of money amounts to Rs12,000 in 5 years and Rs14,400 in 9 years at simple interest. What is the principal?
How much will the interest be for Rs. 1900 at 9% per annum for 3 years?
Anita took a loan of Rs8,000 at a simple interest rate of 7% per annum for 3 years. What will be the simple interest and the total amount she has to repay after 3 years?
Aman invested Rs6,000 in a scheme that gives 5% simple interest per annum. What will be the interest earned and the total amount after 5 years?
A sum becomes Rs. 11,650 in 5 years and Rs. 12,076 in 6 years at simple interest. What is the sum?
A sum doubles in 10 years at SI. find its Rate of interest
A sum of money at simple interest doubles in 6 years. It will become three times in:
A lends $5000 at 5% for 4 years. B lends $4000 at 6% for 3 years. Who earns more SI?
A sum of money at simple interest doubles in 8 years. It will become four times in:
The principal is 1600, the interest rate is 8%, and the time is 5 years. What will be the simple interest?
What will be the ratio of simple interest earned by a certain amount at the same rate of interest for 14 years and 18 years?
A sum of money amounts to Rs. 800 in 3 years and to Rs. 920 in 4 years at some rate of simple interest. The sum is how much?
Ravi borrowed $5,000 from a bank at an interest rate of 6% per annum for 4 years. Calculate the simple interest and the total amount he has to repay after 4 years.
At a local credit society, a deposit grows to 750 in 2 years and Rs900 in 4 years at simple interest. What was the initial principal amount?
A small business owner takes a short-term loan of Rs. 25,000 at 10% simple interest. If the loan is settled 18 months later, what is the total amount needed to clear the debt?
A man took a loan of Rs1200 and paid 432 as simple interest in 3 years. What was the rate of interest?
A saver places money at 15% simple interest per annum. In how many years will his investment double?
A deposit grows to Rs. 650 in 2 years and to Rs. 700 in 3 years under simple interest. What was the original principal?
A man invests Rs 1500 at 12% simple interest. How many years will it take for the interest to amount to Rs 900?
A deposit grows to Rs. 4500 after 3 years at 10% simple interest. What was the principal?
A deposit grows so that the simple interest earned equals three times the principal at 15% per annum. After how many years does this happen?
A shopkeeper borrows Rs2000 for 2.5 years at 12% simple interest per annum. How much interest will he pay?
A person lends Rs480 at 10% simple interest per annum. What interest will he earn in 3 years?
A person earned Rs480 as simple interest in 2 years at 12% per annum. What was the original sum invested?
A shopkeeper invests Rs. 500 in a chit fund that offers 8% simple interest. What interest will he get after 1 year?
A girl invests Rs. 720 for 2 years and earns Rs. 144 as interest. What was the annual rate of simple interest?
A sum of money doubles itself in 10 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum?
A man's deposit amounts to Rs2500 after 3 years at 8% simple interest per annum. What was the initial principal?
A farmer takes a loan of Rs200 from a cooperative bank at 6% simple interest per annum. How much interest will he pay after 2 years?
A man earns Rs 192 as interest in 4 years at 12% simple interest per annum. What was his principal amount?
An investor receives Rs800 as simple interest in 8 years at 4% per annum. What was the principal amount?
A loan yields 2800 as simple interest in 4 years at 7% per annum, How much was lent originally?
A depositor receives Rs180 as simple interest over 3 years at 5% per annum. What was the principal he had invested?
Ramesh puts Rs1000 in a savings account that gives 5% simple interest per annum. How much interest will he earn in 1 year?
A shopkeeper paid Rs540 as interest on a loan taken at 9% simple interest for 3 years. What was the loan amount?
A depositor received 1600 as simple interest over 5 years at 8% per annum. What was the principal?
A borrower paid as simple interest on a loan of over 3 years. What was the annual rate of interest?
A student borrows from a lending club at 9% simple interest per annum. What interest does he need to pay in 1 year?
A landowner lends Rs4000 at a simple interest of 20% per annum. After how many years will the interest amount to Rs3200?
A creditor earns Rs 264 as simple interest in 4 years at 11% per annum. What amount did he lend?
On a principal of Rs1200, the difference between the simple interest for 3 years and for 2 years is Rs96. What was the annual rate of interest?

A total amount is divided into three parts in the ratio 1:2:3. The parts are invested at 5%, 10%, and 15% simple interest, respectively, for one year. If the total interest earned is Rs. 2400, what is the approximate total initial amount?
A borrower pays Rs352 as simple interest on a loan of Rs1600 at 11% per annum. For how many years was the loan taken?
If the simple interest on a principal of Rs. 24,000 over 5 years is Rs. 1,200, what is the annual interest rate?
Rohan took a loan of Rs. 3,20,000 at 11.5% simple interest. After how many years will the simple interest amount to Rs. 18,400?
An amount of money becomes seven times its original value in 20 years with simple interest. Calculate the yearly interest rate.
A sum of money was invested at simple interest at per annum for 6 years. Had the rate of interest been , it would have fetched rs. 168 more. Find the sum invested.

Manish invests two separate amounts of money for 2 years: One part is invested in a government bond that gives 6% simple interest per year. The other part is invested in a company bond that offers 7% simple interest per year. At the end of 2 years, Manish earns a total interest of Rs. 354. Also, one-fourth of the amount invested in the government bond is equal to one-fifth of the amount invested in the company bond. What was the total amount Manish invested?
A sum of Rs4,800 was lent at 6% per annum for 3 years 4 months. Find the simple interest earned.
A sum becomes 8,400 in 3 years and 9,600 in 5 years under simple interest. Find the principal and the annual rate (in % to 3 decimal places).

A sum is divided into three parts in the ratio 2 : 3 : 5. These parts are invested for 4 years at 5% p.a., 6% p.a., and 8% p.a. simple interest respectively. If the total simple interest earned from all three investments after 4 years is 6,800, find the total sum invested.

A lender lends Rs. 10,000 at 8% per annum simple interest for t years and then lends an additional Rs. 5,000 at 12% per annum simple interest for the same period t. If the total simple interest at the end of t years is 5,400, find t (in years).

A man borrowed Rs. 18,000 at 10% per annum simple interest. After 3 years, he repaid the borrowed money along with the interest by giving a motorcycle worth 12,000 and the remaining in cash. Find the amount of cash he paid.
A farmer borrowed Rs10,500 from a village cooperative at 7% simple interest per annum. After 2 years, he repaid the amount along with a processing fee of Rs525. Find the total amount he paid back.

A music teacher borrowed Rs8,200 from a friend at 6.5% simple interest per annum. After 3 years, he repaid the amount along with a small donation of Rs280 to his friend's charity. Find the total amount he paid.

A total sum of 36,000 is divided into two parts. One part is invested at 6% p.a. simple interest for 4 years and the other part is invested at 9% p.a. simple interest for 6 years. If the total simple interest received from both investments (after their respective periods) is 16,020, find the amounts invested at 6% and 9% respectively.

A student borrowed Rs6,400 from his teacher at 7.5% simple interest per annum. He repaid the money after 3 years, along with an additional club membership fee of Rs360. Find the total amount he paid back.

A sum was invested partly at 6% p.a. simple interest and partly at 9% p.a. simple interest. The amount invested at 9% is 2,500 more than the amount invested at 6%. After 3 years, the total simple interest from both parts is 2,070. Find the amount invested at each rate.
A sum lent at simple interest amounts to 13,200 after 2 years and to 15,600 after 5 years. Find the principal and the annual rate of interest.

A shopkeeper borrowed Rs9,000 from a trader at 8% simple interest per annum. After 1.5 years, he repaid the amount along with an additional maintenance charge of Rs240. Find the total amount he paid back.

A total sum of 30,000 is divided into two parts. One part is invested at 7% p.a. simple interest and the other at 11% p.a. simple interest. After 5 years, the total simple interest from both parts is 12,000. Find the amounts invested at 7% and 11% respectively.
A sports coach borrowed Rs12,000 from a welfare fund at 5% simple interest per annum. He returned the amount after 2.5 years, along with a service charge of Rs350. Find the total amount he paid back.

A dance instructor borrowed Rs9,500 from a colleague at 7% simple interest per annum. After 4 years, he repaid the borrowed amount along with an additional donation of Rs350 to his colleague's charity. Find the total amount he paid back.
A librarian borrowed Rs5,500 from a local fund at 9% simple interest per annum. After 4 years, he cleared the loan along with a late penalty of Rs600. Find the total amount he paid in all.

A sum is divided into three parts in the ratio 3 : 4 : 5. The first part is invested at 6% p.a. simple interest for 2 years, the second at 8% p.a. for 3 years, and the third at 10% p.a. for 4 years. If the total simple interest received from the three parts after their respective periods is 8,300, find the total sum invested.

A sum is divided into three parts such that the second part is 2,000 more than the first, and the third part is 5,000 more than the first. The first part is invested at 5% p.a. simple interest for 3 years, the second at 7% p.a. for 4 years, and the third at 9% p.a. for 5 years. If the total simple interest received from the three parts after their respective periods is 4,746, find the three parts.

At a local book club, Arjun lent Rs4,800 to his friend at a simple interest rate of 7.5% per annum. After some time, he received Rs6,000 in total from his friend. Find the time period (in years) for which the money was lent.
The difference between the simple interest on Rs5,000 for 3 years and 4 years at the same rate of interest per annum is Rs500. Find the rate of interest.
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (12th), 02 Aug 2025, Shift 2
